task_completion.description: Create the first graph calculating the absolute difference between the two speed sensors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor for a graph panel shows the use of 'absolute' and 'diffSeries' functions applied to 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1' and 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_2'.

task_completion.description: Set the correct title and alias for the first graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graph panel is titled 'Absolute Speed Divergence' and the legend displays the alias 'Absolute Gap'.

task_completion.description: Create the second graph calculating the performance ratio of sensor 1 to sensor 2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor for a graph panel shows the use of the 'divideSeries' function with 'speed_sensor_1' as the dividend and 'speed_sensor_2' as the divisor.

task_completion.description: Set the correct title and alias for the second graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graph panel is titled 'S1/S2 Performance Ratio' and the legend displays the alias 'S1 to S2 Ratio'.

task_completion.description: Create the third graph calculating a 10-point moving average of the minimum value across both sensors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The query editor for a graph panel shows the use of 'movingAverage' (with a parameter of 10) and 'minSeries' functions applied to both speed sensors.

task_completion.description: Set the correct title and alias for the third graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graph panel is titled 'Smoothed Lower Bound Floor' and the legend displays the alias 'Smoothed Worst Speed'.

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard with the exact requested name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard title at the top of the screen or in the save dialog is exactly 'CDN Routing Divergence'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the monitoring application GUI (e.g., Grafana/Graphite) to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the UI, adding panels, and using the query builder/editor, rather than just running a script in the terminal to generate a JSON dashboard file.

integrity.description: Verify the graphs are actually rendering data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panels should display actual plotted lines or data points, confirming that the metrics are correctly queried from the backend and not just empty mockups.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the expected output into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The active window should be a web browser displaying the monitoring tool, not a text editor or terminal echoing the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Dashboard Name: 'CDN Routing Divergence'. Expected Graph Titles: 'Absolute Speed Divergence', 'S1/S2 Performance Ratio', 'Smoothed Lower Bound Floor'. The task requires using specific series functions (absolute, diffSeries, divideSeries, movingAverage, minSeries) and aliases ('Absolute Gap', 'S1 to S2 Ratio', 'Smoothed Worst Speed') within a monitoring UI.
